title: QUINOLINE-8-CARBOXYLIC ACID AS AN ANALYTICAL REAGENT. PART I.
author: ANIL KUMAR MAJUMDAR
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Quinoline-8-carboxylic acid, has been used as an analytical reagent for the estimation of copper. Copper has been found to be completely precipitated in pH as low as 2.78. In neutral solution copper gives a distinct precipitate even at a dilution of I in 4,000,000 parts.
steps_raw: Quinoline-8-carboxylic acid, has been used as an analytical reagent for the estimation of copper. Copper has been found to be completely precipitated in pH as low as 2.78. In neutral solution copper gives a distinct precipitate even at a dilution of I in 4,000,000 parts.
